title: The AI agent that replaced my entire sales team
post:
I replaced my 8-person sales team with one AI agent.

Six months ago, I would have said this was impossible. Today, our AI agent handles 3x the leads with a 40% higher conversion rate. It never forgets to follow up. It Personalizes every outreach at scale. It works 24/7 without burnout.

The team wasn't happy. I wasn't either—at first.

But here's what I learned: they weren't replaced. They were freed to do the relationship-building work that actually required humans. The AI handles the tedious, repetitive outreach. My (smaller) team handles the deals that need trust and nuance.

This isn't about cutting costs. It's about letting humans do human work.

What's your take on AI replacing sales roles?
